# NeurIPS 2025

**Coverage status:** Pending

**Checked on:** 2026-07-13

**Cataloged eligible papers:** 4

**Tracks checked:** Main

**Tracks pending:** Main (partially screened: Main proceedings list was keyword-screened and four candidates were paper-level verified, but a full abstract-level adjudication remains open.) · Workshop (source mapped: Workshop or organizer paper roster was source-mapped but not fully screened at paper level.) · Affinity (source mapped: Affinity-event or poster roster was source-mapped but not fully screened at paper level.)

**Official audit sources:** [Source 1](<https://neurips.cc/virtual/2025/papers.html>) · [Source 2](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper/2025>) · [Source 3](<https://neurips.cc/Conferences/2025/DatasetsBenchmarks/AcceptedPapers>) · [Source 4](<https://neurips.cc/virtual/2025/events/workshop>) · [Source 5](<https://neurips.cc/virtual/2025/affinity_events>)

**Coverage notes:** The official proceedings and virtual paper lists were re-screened on 2026-07-13 with finance, market, portfolio, trading, volatility, asset, and related terms, then candidate abstracts were checked at paper level. Four eligible main papers were cataloged: Robust Reinforcement Learning in Finance, OPHR, TwinMarket, and Online Portfolio Selection with ML Predictions. PHANTOM and central-bank-communication candidates were excluded because they are financial QA/text-analysis resources rather than direct quant trading, risk, execution, or asset-management methods. Main remains partial rather than complete because this was not a full abstract-level adjudication of every accepted paper; workshop and affinity rosters remain pending.

4 verified papers curated for direct relevance to quantitative finance and asset management. Tracks are separated so main-conference, workshop, position, and affinity status remains visible.

## Main Conference (4)

### [Online Portfolio Selection with ML Predictions](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/file/76981cb7a06b5ad53639f5bbe9924388-Paper-Conference.pdf>)

**Catalog ID:** 2025-neurips-zhang-online-portfolio-selection

**Authors:** Ziliang Zhang, Tianming Zhao, Albert Zomaya

**Venue / year:** NeurIPS · 2025

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-13

**Topics:** Portfolio Optimization · Asset Allocation · Alpha Modeling · Risk Management

**Assets / frequency:** Equities

**Tasks:** online portfolio selection · learning-augmented allocation

**Focus:** Formalizes online portfolio selection with possibly wrong ML predictions and proposes an allocation rule with guarantees under perfect and adversarial forecasts.

**Why it matters:** Shows how modest predictive signals can be converted into wealth growth without discarding worst-case portfolio-selection guarantees.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/hash/76981cb7a06b5ad53639f5bbe9924388-Abstract-Conference.html>) · [Paper](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/file/76981cb7a06b5ad53639f5bbe9924388-Paper-Conference.pdf>)

---

### [OPHR: Mastering Volatility Trading with Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/file/4c7dbef8023a15c4b81dc95a6ea08bf3-Paper-Conference.pdf>)

**Catalog ID:** 2025-neurips-chen-ophr-volatility-trading

**Authors:** Zeting Chen, Xinyu Cai, Molei Qin, Bo An

**Venue / year:** NeurIPS · 2025

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-13

**Topics:** Derivatives · Alpha Modeling · Financial Agents · Risk Management

**Assets / frequency:** Derivatives, Crypto

**Tasks:** volatility trading · options hedging

**Focus:** Uses a multi-agent reinforcement-learning architecture to time long/short volatility positions and route hedging strategies in cryptocurrency options.

**Why it matters:** Converts volatility forecasts into explicit option-trading and hedging decisions evaluated by profit and risk-adjusted performance.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/hash/4c7dbef8023a15c4b81dc95a6ea08bf3-Abstract-Conference.html>) · [Paper](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/file/4c7dbef8023a15c4b81dc95a6ea08bf3-Paper-Conference.pdf>) · [Code](<https://github.com/Edwicn/OPHR-MasteringVolatilityTradingwithMultiAgentDeepReinforcementLearning>)

---

### [Robust Reinforcement Learning in Finance: Modeling Market Impact with Elliptic Uncertainty Sets](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/file/3c129892b4f9c8326aba665425a470c5-Paper-Conference.pdf>)

**Catalog ID:** 2025-neurips-ma-robust-rl-market-impact

**Authors:** Shaocong Ma, Heng Huang

**Venue / year:** NeurIPS · 2025

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-13

**Topics:** Execution · Risk Management · Financial Agents

**Assets / frequency:** Multi Asset

**Tasks:** market-impact-aware trading · robust reinforcement-learning policy evaluation

**Focus:** Models market impact as asymmetric uncertainty and evaluates robust reinforcement-learning traders on single-asset and multi-asset trading tasks.

**Why it matters:** Addresses the live-trading gap where a strategy's own orders move prices, improving robustness under larger trade volumes.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/hash/3c129892b4f9c8326aba665425a470c5-Abstract-Conference.html>) · [Paper](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/file/3c129892b4f9c8326aba665425a470c5-Paper-Conference.pdf>)

---

### [TwinMarket: A Scalable Behavioral and Social Simulation for Financial Markets](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/file/5bf234ecf83cd77bc5b77a24ba9338b0-Paper-Conference.pdf>)

**Catalog ID:** 2025-neurips-yang-twinmarket

**Authors:** Yuzhe YANG, Yifei Zhang, Minghao Wu, Kaidi Zhang, Yunmiao Zhang, Honghai Yu, Yan Hu, Benyou Wang

**Venue / year:** NeurIPS · 2025

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-13

**Topics:** Market Simulation · Synthetic Data · Financial Agents · Market Regimes

**Assets / frequency:** Equities

**Tasks:** stock-market simulation · behavioral market scenario analysis

**Focus:** Simulates behavioral and social interactions among LLM agents in a stock-market environment to study bubbles, recessions, and other emergent market outcomes.

**Why it matters:** Provides a scalable experimental environment for stress-testing market dynamics that arise from interacting investor behaviors.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/hash/5bf234ecf83cd77bc5b77a24ba9338b0-Abstract-Conference.html>) · [Paper](<https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/file/5bf234ecf83cd77bc5b77a24ba9338b0-Paper-Conference.pdf>)
